steps.title: Read the contrast signal
steps.content: The sentence sets up a paired opposition: a reign could be considered "historically significant" or, conversely, relatively ______. The word conversely demands an antonym of "historically significant" — something meaning unimportant or without historical weight.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'momentous': Means of great importance — this reinforces "historically significant" rather than contrasting it; conversely demands the opposite. ✗
  • Choice B — 'inconsequential': Means lacking importance or significance — the direct antonym of "historically significant," exactly what conversely signals. ✓
  • Choice C — 'benevolent': Means kind and generous — describes a ruler's character, not the historical weight of a reign; no contrast with "significant" is established. ✗
  • Choice D — 'genuine': Means authentic or real — describes authenticity, not historical importance; does not contrast with "significant." ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
